title: Reference Rates - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York publishes multiple reference rates for money markets, including the Effective Federal Funds Rate (EFFR), Overnight Bank Funding Rate (OBFR), Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR), Broad General Collateral Rate (BGCR), and Tri-Party General Collateral Rate (TGCR). These rates provide transparent, independent pricing sources that facilitate standardized contracts, reduce information asymmetries, and support robust market functioning. The New York Fed has served as an administrator and producer of reference rates since the 1950s and maintains compliance with IOSCO's Principles for Financial Benchmarks.
key_points: EFFR is calculated from overnight federal funds transactions reported by domestic banks and U.S. branches of foreign banks via the FR 2420 Report
OBFR extends EFFR data to include overnight Eurodollar transactions and certain deposit transactions at domestic branches
SOFR measures the general cost of financing Treasury securities overnight, combining BGCR data with FICC Delivery-versus-Payment repo transactions
SOFR Averages are backward-looking compounded rates with 30-, 90-, and 180-calendar day tenors; SOFR Index began April 2, 2018
BGCR covers overnight Treasury general collateral repo including tri-party and FICC GCF repo transactions
TGCR specifically measures overnight tri-party GC repo secured by Treasury securities between identified counterparties
New York Fed reference rates are compliant with IOSCO Principles for Financial Benchmarks
why_it_matters: Reference rates form critical market infrastructure by providing transparent pricing benchmarks that support efficient market functioning. They reduce information asymmetries, lower transaction costs through standardized contracts, and can limit manipulation incentives when robustly designed.
macro_context: Reference rate infrastructure supports monetary policy implementation and provides pricing benchmarks used in money market transactions and contract settlement.
market_structure_context: This source documents the institutional framework behind key money market reference rates (EFFR, OBFR, SOFR, BGCR, TGCR) that serve as pricing references for various market participants and transactions.
